judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports cover all four expectations well; Report 2 is crisper that the pipeline literally has no migration step, while Report 1 is firmer on removing the legacy secret — overall comparable.

report_1.evidence: `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:4` `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"` — a QA test account pre-provisioned with superadmin privileges ... Should be **removed entirely** from prod. ... `SHADOW_SCORING_SINK = "s3://medislot-shadow/staging/observations/"` ... Both keys should be **omitted entirely** from prod ... `LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN = "arn:synthetic:secrets:legacy-billing-key"` ... Should be **removed entirely**

report_2.evidence: `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:4` — `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"` ... it should not exist in prod at all — the value should be removed/emptied ... blank out `SHADOW_SCORING_SINK` entirely, since the enabled flag is not load-bearing in the code

report_1.evidence: this is a compile-time substitution, not something read at runtime ... The **same digest** — the same already-built image containing the staging API URL — is what `deploy-prod` deploys ... the production deployment would ship a frontend bundle whose JS literally points API calls at `https://staging-api.example.com` ... This is the exact "rebuild per environment, don't promote a build artifact wholesale" trap

report_2.evidence: the production frontend, as this pipeline is currently written, would ship with `VITE_API_BASE_URL=https://staging-api.example.com` baked into its JS bundle — production users' browsers would call the staging API ... the identical container image, built once with the staging-mode bundle, is deployed to both environments

report_1.evidence: `docs/prod-runbook.md:6` asserts migrations "are applied automatically during deploy," but with `deploy.sh` missing, this can't be confirmed ... this needs to be explicitly confirmed (not assumed) before the first release, especially since this is the *first* production deploy and there's no existing schema to fall back on

report_2.evidence: **No database migration step.** `docs/prod-runbook.md:6` asserts "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but nothing in `deploy.yml` runs Alembic or any migration tool ... the runbook is simply inaccurate and prod will launch with an unmigrated schema

report_1.evidence: `infra/prod/main.tf:3` still has `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"` ... the production deploy role's trust policy still only trusts the **staging** GitHub Environment's OIDC identity. This must be changed to `"repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production"` (a distinct, prod-scoped condition, never shared/OR'd with staging's)

report_2.evidence: `infra/prod/main.tf:3` — `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"`. The production IAM deploy role's trust policy (via `infra/modules/deploy_role/main.tf:8-10`) is scoped to GitHub OIDC tokens carrying the **staging** environment claim ... This must be changed to `"repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production"`
